title: Measuring public opinion
description: Test your skills in this quiz on measuring public opinion.

assessment_items.question_content:
Before her candidate announces his bid for office, a campaign manager organizes a short survey of likely voters to determine which issues most concern them. She hopes that by conducting this survey before the campaign starts, she can tell her candidate the likelihood of winning based on his position on key issues.

**Which type of poll is this campaign manager conducting? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

On Election Day, radio news network Guam Live is trying to project the result of the presidential election. They send pollsters to 350 voting places throughout the United States to survey people after they have voted. 

**Which type of poll is the news network conducting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

On Election Day, TV news network Houston News One is trying to project the 2018 Texas election results. They send pollsters to 400 voting places throughout the state to survey people before they cast their vote.

**Which type of poll is the news network conducting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is a characteristic of a reliable scientific poll?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is a characteristic of a reliable scientific poll?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is a characteristic of a reliable scientific poll?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A recent poll indicates that 75 percent of all people in the United States support new gun control legislation.

**Which of the following supports the credibility of the poll result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The poll reported a sampling error of plus or minus 2.5 percent
The poll was conducted on a major television network and open to anyone who phoned in to participate
The poll was administered to several thousand people who were stopped while shopping in Kroger grocery stores across the country
The poll was given to 150 people of all ages and races

assessment_items.question_content:
A recent poll indicates that 57 percent of all people in the United States disapprove of the Supreme Court’s ruling that no state or local government may require the reading of Bible verses in public schools.

**Which of the following supports the credibility of the poll result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The poll received a representative sample of 1,850 respondents
The poll was administered to people who were walking out of religious organizations across the country
The poll did not report a sampling error on their results
The poll was conducted on Twitter and open to anyone who wished to participate

assessment_items.question_content:
A recent poll indicates that 89 percent of US adults favor expanding solar panel farms throughout the United States.

**Which of the following supports the credibility of the poll result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The poll was conducted over the phone using a random-digit-dialing system
The poll did not report a sampling error on their results
The poll adjusted their results after finding that they had more Democratic respondents than Republican respondents
The poll was conducted on Twitter and open to anyone who wished to participate

assessment_items.question_content:
Count the Votes, a website that focuses on opinion poll analysis, conducts a poll every day for four years to determine how popular the sitting president is with likely or registered voters.

**Which type of poll is Count the Votes conducting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a public opinion poll of a representative sample of a population, 1,800 people were asked to agree, disagree, or express neutrality toward the following statements. 

Statement A: The government should do more to protect air quality and water quality for American citizens.

Result: 51 percent of the respondents agreed. 

Statement B: The government should punish companies who harm the environment with their business practices. 

Result: 20 percent of the respondents agreed. 

**Which of the following most likely accounts for the different results?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The questions contained a difference in wording
The sample size was too large and no longer representative
The sample size was not the entire population

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best explains why a Congressional candidate might use a focus group over a telephone survey?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Focus groups are more useful to determine why people have the opinions they do than telephone surveys
Focus groups usually have more respondents than telephone surveys
Focus groups generally have more diverse respondents than telephone surveys
Focus groups are typically more reliable sources of data than telephone surveys
